key_metric: 3x more committed code across 30,000 developers
spokesperson: Wei Luo, VP of Engineering; Fabian Theuring, Senior Software Architect
summary: NVIDIA embedded Cursor across every phase of its SDLC for 30,000+ daily users, driving a 3x increase in committed code while maintaining flat bug rates. Teams automated entire workflows including git flow, CI debugging, and bug fixes using custom rules and MCP servers.

key_quotes.text: Cursor is used in pretty much all product areas and in all aspects of software development.
key_quotes.author: Wei Luo
key_quotes.title: VP of Engineering, NVIDIA

key_quotes.text: We have built a lot of custom rules in Cursor to fully automate entire workflows. That has unlocked Cursor's true potential.
key_quotes.author: Fabian Theuring
key_quotes.title: Senior Software Architect, NVIDIA

key_metric: 30%+ velocity acceleration with double-digit gains in quality
summary: Salesforce saw double-digit gains in cycle time, quality (bug count), and throughput after adopting Cursor across thousands of engineers. One team reduced legacy code coverage time by 85%. Junior engineers used Cursor to onboard faster during remote work, while senior engineers started with tedious tasks and quickly expanded to higher-value use cases.

key_quotes.text: It's 0 to 1 in terms of how Cursor has transformed the way our developers use tools to improve quality of the product.
key_quotes.author: Shan Appajodu
key_quotes.title: SVP of Engineering, Salesforce

key_quotes.text: We have seen more than double-digit increases in all of these metrics.
key_quotes.author: Shan Appajodu
key_quotes.title: SVP of Engineering, Salesforce

key_metric: Consistent AI rollout across 3,000 engineers with 5-year-high developer sentiment
spokesperson: Patrick Collison, Co-Founder & CEO; Scott MacVicar, Head of Developer Infrastructure
summary: Stripe rolled out Cursor to 3,000+ engineers by preloading it on every machine, using Cursor Rules for codebase context, and adapting code review for AI-generated code. Longest-tenured engineers saw the biggest gains. Developer sentiment hit a five-year high.

key_quotes.text: Cursor quickly grew from hundreds to thousands of extremely enthusiastic Stripe employees.
key_quotes.author: Patrick Collison
key_quotes.title: Co-Founder & CEO, Stripe

key_quotes.text: My advice for anyone thinking about trying AI coding is, move faster on this.
key_quotes.author: Scott MacVicar
key_quotes.title: Head of Developer Infrastructure, Stripe

key_metric: 30-50% improvement in roadmap delivery; 80-90% faster migrations
spokesperson: Aaron Levie, CEO; Swaroop Butala, Senior Director of Engineering; Mick Ryan, Staff Engineer
summary: Box selected Cursor for its enterprise-grade security and data privacy controls. Over 85% of developers use Cursor daily, driving 30-50% more roadmap throughput. A stalled React migration was completed 80% faster than expected, and a design system migration finished 90% faster. Box also built a formal mentorship program that boosted Cursor usage by 75%.

key_quotes.text: It became obvious that Cursor is one of the clear leaders to bet on.
key_quotes.author: Aaron Levie
key_quotes.title: CEO, Box

key_quotes.text: Before Cursor, this migration would have taken a developer a full quarter. We are now wrapping up the project in about a week.
key_quotes.author: Swaroop Butala
key_quotes.title: Senior Director of Engineering, Box

company_size: Thousands of engineers (90%+ weekly AI usage)
key_metric: 1M+ lines of AI-suggested code accepted monthly; 550K+ files indexed
summary: Dropbox engineers accept over one million lines of AI-suggested code monthly. Cursor indexed Dropbox's 550,000+ file monorepo, enabling semantic reasoning across the entire codebase. Over 90% of engineers use AI tools weekly. Adoption grew organically through AI champions and a company-wide hackathon.

key_quotes.text: Speed is the only advantage of any company.
key_quotes.author: Ali Dasdan
key_quotes.title: CTO, Dropbox

key_quotes.text: I waited until the last minute because I trusted the tool, and it still let me finish the whole thing in about two hours.
key_quotes.author: Ali Dasdan
key_quotes.title: CTO, Dropbox

key_metric: 80% of Bugbot comments addressed before merge; equivalent of 2 FTE saved
spokesperson: Fatih Arslan, Software Engineer; Sam Lambert, CEO
summary: As coding agents made code generation cheap and fast, code review became PlanetScale's bottleneck. Bugbot now reviews 2,000+ PRs monthly, catching deep semantic bugs that could cause production downtime. Roughly 80% of Bugbot comments are addressed before merge.

key_quotes.text: Bugbot is different from other tools. It detects issues that as a human reviewer I would never imagine to look at. I was blown away.
key_quotes.author: Fatih Arslan
key_quotes.title: Software Engineer, PlanetScale

key_quotes.text: If I took Bugbot away from our engineering team, there would be a mutiny.
key_quotes.author: Sam Lambert
key_quotes.title: CEO, PlanetScale

key_metric: 15-20 hours saved per developer per week; QA test generation 70% faster
spokesperson: Aaron Li, Staff Engineer; Tran Ba Vinh Son, Group Company CTO; Xie Lester, Director in Chief Quality Office
summary: Money Forward expanded Cursor from engineering to product, design, and QA teams. Over 1,000 employees use Cursor daily. Engineers save 15-20 hours/week, QA generates test cases 70% faster using Jira/Notion MCPs.

key_quotes.text: We held an engineering all-hands where we showed that Cursor's agents could actually tackle entire software engineering tasks from end-to-end. The bottoms-up demand from developers was immediate.
key_quotes.author: Aaron Li
key_quotes.title: Staff Engineer, Money Forward

key_quotes.text: The QA team now uses Cursor to analyze incidents, automate test results, and review specs before development.
key_quotes.author: Xie Lester
key_quotes.title: Director in the Chief Quality Office, Money Forward

company_size: 6,000+ engineers and technologists (using Cursor or Q Developer)
key_metric: 40x leap in generating software requirements; 5-6x uplift in development phases
spokesperson: Nicholas Di Giuseppe, Executive for Transformation Engineering; Kimberley Kam, UX Designer; Pete Steel, Group Executive Digital, Data & AI; Patrick Wright, Group Executive Technology
summary: NAB saw staggering results from Cursor trials: 40x faster software requirements generation, 5-6x development uplift, push notifications added 5x faster, a referral app prototype built in under 10 minutes, and 6 months of merchant onboarding work completed in 2.5 days. UX designers use Cursor to convert design concepts into clickable prototypes in hours instead of days.

key_quotes.text: We've seen staggering improvements so far. A 40x leap in generating software requirements and a 5-6x uplift in development phases.
key_quotes.author: Nicholas Di Giuseppe
key_quotes.title: Executive for Transformation Engineering, NAB

key_quotes.text: It's amazing. I'm able to instantly turn a design concept into clickable prototypes, so I can get buy-in and start testing and refining with customers. That's days of work in just hours!
key_quotes.author: Kimberley Kam
key_quotes.title: UX Designer, NAB

key_metric: 92% adoption rate; ~40% of code AI-generated; PR review cycle reduced from 150 to 15 hours (10x)
spokesperson: Brian Armstrong, CEO; Chintan Turakhia, Senior Director of Engineering
summary: By February 2025, every Coinbase engineer had utilized Cursor, which became the preferred IDE. Single engineers now refactor, upgrade, or build new codebases in days instead of months. The famous 'PR speed run' saw 800 engineers submit 400 PRs in 30 minutes. 92% adoption rate with 40% of code now AI-generated.

key_quotes.text: By February 2025, every Coinbase engineer had utilized Cursor, which has become the preferred IDE for most of our developers. Single engineers are now refactoring, upgrading, or building new codebases in days instead of months.
key_quotes.author: Brian Armstrong
key_quotes.title: CEO, Coinbase

key_metric: 70%+ engineers using Cursor; 45% of code changes fully AI-written
summary: Over 70% of Brex engineers use Cursor, making it the standout tool despite a philosophy of letting engineers choose their own tools. Over 45% of code changes are now fully written by AI. Gains seen in day-to-day development, large-scale migrations, debugging, and onboarding.

key_quotes.text: More than 70% of our engineers now use Cursor, and we've seen meaningful gains in day-to-day development, faster execution on large-scale migrations, increased rate of debugging, and even faster onboarding.
key_quotes.author: James Reggio
key_quotes.title: CTO, Brex

key_metric: Adoption grew from 150 to 500+ engineers in weeks; clear gains in code quality and velocity
summary: Rippling's Cursor adoption started with a single curious engineer nine months ago and grew from 150 to over 500 engineers (~60% of the org) in just a few weeks. The company created SPARK forums where early adopters share workflows. Clear gains in code quality, velocity, and onboarding speed.

key_quotes.text: Cursor has transformed the way our engineering teams write and ship code, with adoption growing from 150 to over 500 engineers (~60% of our org!) in just a few weeks.
key_quotes.author: Albert Strasheim
key_quotes.title: CTO, Rippling

key_metric: 25% increase in PR volume; 100% increase in average PR size; ~50% more code shipped
summary: Upwork achieved nearly 100% Cursor adoption vs. GitHub Copilot's prior 20% adoption rate. 70% of engineers save at least one hour weekly. Combined 25% more PRs at 2x average size equals roughly 50% more code shipped overall.

key_quotes.text: Across roles and levels, we're seeing an increase of over 25% in PR volume and over 100% in the average PR size. Together, that means we're shipping about 50% more code.
key_quotes.author: Anton Andreev
key_quotes.title: Principal Software Engineer, Upwork

key_metric: Dozen agent branches merging daily; scale-multiplier for the whole org
summary: At Sentry, watching a dozen agent branches merge every day has become normal. The freed-up velocity shows up in release cadence and bug-backlog burn-down. Engineers use background agents to implement features in unfamiliar languages (e.g., C# parser support with zero prior C# experience).

key_quotes.text: Watching a dozen agent branches merge every day has become normal, and that freed-up velocity shows up everywhere from release cadence to bug-backlog burn-down. Cursor isn't a convenience add-on; it's a scale-multiplier for the whole org.
key_quotes.author: Cody De Arkland
key_quotes.title: Senior Director, Sentry

key_metric: Coding agents improve both speed and code quality
summary: Datadog's CTO calls coding agents like Cursor the killer app for AI. Not only do they increase code creation speed, they also improve code quality. Datadog also integrates with Cursor via MCP for debugging workflows.

key_quotes.text: Coding agents like Cursor have become the killer app for AI. Not only do coding agents increase the speed at which code is created, they also improve code quality.
key_quotes.author: Alexis Le-Quoc
key_quotes.title: CTO & Co-Founder, Datadog

key_metric: 2-5x increase in engineering velocity
spokesperson: Roni Avidov, Senior R&D Team Lead
summary: monday.com saw a 2-5x increase in engineering velocity with Cursor, along with better handling of tech debt, code refactors, unit testing, and the ability to prototype ideas in hours instead of weeks. Their R&D team lead converted from being 'team IntelliJ for life.'

key_quotes.text: I was team IntelliJ for life and am now one of Cursor's top fans. Across the board, we've seen a 2-5x increase in engineering velocity, better handling of tech debt, code refactors, unit testing, and the ability to prototype ideas in hours instead of weeks.
key_quotes.author: Roni Avidov
key_quotes.title: Senior R&D Team Lead, monday.com

key_metric: 800+ engineers deployed; 99% say Cursor made them more productive
spokesperson: Jonah McIntire, Chief Product & Technology Officer
summary: Trimble rolled out Cursor to over 800 engineers across diverse technology stacks, from edge computing on hardware to multi-tenant marketplaces. Over 99% of engineers reported that Cursor made them more productive and outperformed previous AI tooling. By the end of the pilot, engineers from every product line were utilizing Cursor.

key_quotes.text: We've rolled Cursor out to over 800 engineers and are seeing significant output lift in how teams plan and execute code changes. Whether you're building new applications or refactoring legacy systems, I would highly recommend Cursor to other CTOs.
key_quotes.author: Jonah McIntire
key_quotes.title: Chief Product & Technology Officer, Trimble

key_metric: CTO reports unprecedented employee gratitude for the technology
summary: Fox's CTO describes the cultural impact of bringing Cursor to teams: employees texting and Slacking 'Thank you for getting this technology here.' Fox uses a bottom-up adoption model where teams learn from each other rather than top-down mandates, with AI applied across creating stories, curating content, and carrying out repetitive tasks.

key_quotes.text: I've never, as a CTO, received so many texts or Slack messages from employees just saying 'Thank you for getting this technology here.'
key_quotes.author: Melody Hildebrandt
key_quotes.title: CTO, Fox

key_metric: Used by OpenAI's own engineering team; 'we are at the 1% of what's possible'
summary: OpenAI's President describes Cursor as making programming more fun -- less about digging through pages, more about what you want to happen. He believes interactive experiences like Cursor are where frontier models shine brightest and that we are only at 1% of what is possible.

key_quotes.text: It's definitely becoming more fun to be a programmer. It's less about digging through pages and more about what you want to happen. We are at the 1% of what's possible.
key_quotes.author: Greg Brockman
key_quotes.title: President, OpenAI

key_metric: Transforms how software is created
spokesperson: Bret Taylor, Co-Founder & CEO (former Salesforce CEO)
summary: Bret Taylor, former Salesforce CEO and co-founder of Sierra, describes Cursor as transforming how he creates software, calling it a tool that enhances his life's passion of building software.

key_quotes.text: I'm really a big fan of Cursor. I've enjoyed taking something I love and has been my life's passion and seeing how this AI tool transforms how I create software.
key_quotes.author: Bret Taylor
key_quotes.title: Co-Founder & CEO, Sierra

key_metric: Features built in a day that would otherwise take weeks to prototype
summary: Mercado Libre sees features built in a day that might otherwise have taken weeks to prototype. Cursor helps teams ramp up on unfamiliar codebases, generate meaningful code, and debug complex issues with more speed and confidence.

key_quotes.text: In certain cases, we've seen features built in a day that might otherwise have taken weeks to prototype. It helps teams ramp up on unfamiliar codebases, generate meaningful code, and debug complex issues with more speed and confidence.
key_quotes.author: Oscar Mullin
key_quotes.title: VP of Technology, Mercado Libre

key_metric: Indispensable daily workflow tool; enables curiosity in new problem spaces
summary: eBay engineers describe Cursor as not just a productivity tool but one that makes them more curious and confident to explore new problem spaces. It has become an indispensable part of daily workflows.

key_quotes.text: Cursor has helped me not only be more productive but also more curious and confident to explore new problem spaces. It's genuinely become an indispensable tool in my daily workflow.
key_quotes.author: Lathesh Karkera
key_quotes.title: Software Engineer, eBay

key_metric: Exceptional debugging with historical code change attribution; faster new joiner ramp
summary: OnePay's CTO describes Cursor as the popular IDE 'on steroids' -- exceptional at debugging, attributing issues to precise historical code changes, writing documentation, and helping new joiners ramp up faster.

key_quotes.text: Cursor took the most popular IDE in the world and put it on steroids. It's exceptional at debugging issues and attributing them to precise historical code changes, has stellar writing documentation skills, and has been incredibly helpful for new joiners.
key_quotes.author: Moe Matar
key_quotes.title: CTO, OnePay

key_metric: Firm-wide deployment at a global trading firm; robust at scale and complexity
summary: Optiver expanded Cursor to firm-wide deployment across a global trading firm where almost everyone writes code. Teams find Cursor robust, context-aware, and flexible enough to support the scale and complexity of global trading operations.

key_quotes.text: At Optiver, we've recently expanded our Cursor usage to firm-wide deployment. Our teams have consistently found Cursor to be robust, context-aware, and flexible enough to support the scale and complexity of a global trading firm.
key_quotes.author: Scott McKenzie
key_quotes.title: Head of Engineering, Optiver

key_metric: 100% engineering adoption; essential tool for navigating complexity
summary: Decagon achieved 100% adoption across their engineering team. Cursor is the tool every engineer, including the co-founder, instinctively turns to when navigating complexity or hitting a wall.

key_quotes.text: Cursor is the tool that every engineer (including me) instinctively turns to when navigating complexity or hitting a wall. With 100% adoption across our engineering team, Cursor has become an essential part of how we build.
key_quotes.author: Ashwin Sreenivas
key_quotes.title: Co-Founder, Decagon

key_metric: ~30% delivery velocity improvement; 25% defect density reduction; 50% faster onboarding; report build time cut from 12 to <7 hours
summary: R Systems established a dedicated Co-Innovation Lab to deploy Cursor across 1,000+ AI-focused engineers with a reusable AI-SDLC Framework. One project migrated 45 complex reports into Vue.js dashboards in 8 weeks, cutting average report build time from ~12 to <7 hours and doubling engineering velocity.

key_metric: Engineering teams across portfolio accelerating product roadmaps
summary: The Carlyle Group sees engineering teams across its portfolio and internally using Cursor to accelerate product roadmaps, ship features faster, and handle demand surges while maintaining quality. They highlight the ability to start small and scale as a differentiator.

key_quotes.text: We're seeing engineering teams in our portfolio and at Carlyle use Cursor to accelerate product roadmaps, ship features faster, and handle surges in demand, all while maintaining quality and focus.
key_quotes.author: Daniel Ducic
key_quotes.title: Investor, Carlyle
